title: GRAPHIC USER INTERFACES FOR ABLATION SYSTEMS
abstract: A computer-implemented method for generating and displaying a graphical user interface (GUI) is disclosed. The method includes displaying, via the GUI, a real-time video received from a camera disposed within an ablation catheter. The method further includes displaying, via the GUI, a graphical representation of a plurality of electrodes of the ablation catheter.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. A computer-implemented method for generating and displaying a graphical user interface (GUI), the method comprising: displaying, via the GUI, a real-time video received from a camera disposed within an ablation catheter; and displaying, via the GUI, a graphical representation of a plurality of electrodes of the ablation catheter. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the GUI displays the real-time video in a first circular region, and wherein the GUI displays the graphical representation in a second circular region. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first circular region and the second circular region are positioned adjacent to each other on the GUI. 4. The method of any of claims 1-3, wherein the displaying the real-time video includes displaying video received from a plurality of cameras disposed within the ablation catheter. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein the graphical representation includes an electrode icon for each of the plurality of electrodes of the ablation catheter. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising: displaying real-time electrical impedance within selected electrode icons. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the displayed real-time electrical impedance is based on electrical impedance sensed by the ablation electrodes. 8. The method of any of claims 5-7, further comprising: displaying, via the GUI, highlighted electrode icons; and assigning each highlighted electrode icon as being a source electrode or a sink electrode. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein only the electrode icons associated with a source electrode display electrical impedance. 10. The method of any of claims 1-9, further comprising: displaying, via the GUI, respective icons for controlling irrigation fluid flow rate, illumination power, and ablation electrode power. 1 1. The method of any of claims 1-10, wherein the graphical representation includes an electrode icon corresponding to each of the electrodes of the ablation catheter. 12. The method of any of claims 1-1 1 , further comprising: displaying icons associated with functions of the real-time video adjacent the real-time video; and displaying icons associated with functions of the graphical representation adjacent the real-time graphical representation. 13. A computing device adapted to execute the steps of the method of claims 1-12. 14. A computer program product comprising instructions to cause one or more processors to carry out the steps of the method of claims 1-12. 15. A computer-readable medium having stored thereon the computer program product of claim 14.
